methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $170M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $1.8B.

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: The Bot Company
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Telexistence, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Jin Tomioka and Ken Tomoyose, has raised $170M+ to date. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private peers—Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B Series B), Hadrian ($1.6B Series C), and Nimble Robotics ($1.0B Series C)—alongside one public robotics comparable.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $170M total raised as a proxy base value and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $323M. Peer-median analysis across the four robotics comparables yields a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range of $1.0B to $1.8B. These divergent signals reflect the significant heterogeneity within early-stage robotics valuations and stage-specific funding dynamics.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $275M (low) to $1.9B (high), with a median of $974M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around market positioning relative to the peer set, customer traction, and the applicability of later-stage peer valuations to Telexistence's current stage. Resolution of these uncertainties through customer concentration, revenue run rate, and gross margin data would narrow the range materially.
